description_long: This object consists of a group of nine potted plants arranged in three rows, each row containing three identical plants. The plants have long, slender, pointed green leaves with a slight arch, radiating outward from a central base, and some leaves have a subtle variegation with lighter edges. The pots are small, round, and appear to be plain, with no visible decorative elements or patterns. The overall arrangement suggests a uniform, decorative display, suitable for lining walkways or accenting urban spaces. The plants resemble Dracaena or spider plant varieties, commonly used for their hardy nature and visual appeal. The asset is compact and low to the ground, making it ideal for placement on sidewalks, in parks, or near building entrances.
description: A group of nine small potted plants with long, pointed green leaves arranged in three neat rows. The plain pots and uniform foliage make them suitable for urban decorative use.
description_view_0: Front view showing all nine potted plants in three rows, with clear visibility of the leaf arrangement and pot alignment.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the arrangement and the profile of the plants' arching leaves.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the plants and the uniformity of the rows, with some text visible beside the plants.
description_view_3: Angled view from the opposite side, showing the staggered rows and the spread of the leaves from a different perspective.
